principles: Weeks are for sequencing, not perfection.
Each week should move 1–3 major things forward.
Constraints (travel, games, calls) must be accounted for.

playbooks.name: Weekly planning
steps: Clarify the 1–3 big outcomes for the week.
Place deep work blocks around constraints.

constraints: Avoid planning every hour of the week.
Do not ignore known high-friction days.

examples.situation: User wants to overhaul their entire life in one week.
examples.response: Anchor the week around 1–2 major shifts and stabilize them first.
